headline: Here's How Stellantis Is in Even Worse Shape Than Its Rival -- but Clear Upside Remains
summary: Ford catches a lot of heat for its record years of recalls, but what many don't realize is that they're far less costly than Stellantis' recalls.

headline: Tesla Defies Auto Industry Sales Slump, Goldman Raises Q2 Delivery Forecast
summary: Goldman Sachs expects rising Tesla sales at a time when the broader auto industry is struggling.  Goldman's forecast of 420,000 cars would be a 9.3% increase compared to Q2 2025, when Tesla delivered 384,122 vehicles.  Sales growth of that level would buck current trends for both Tesla — which has had two years of declining sales — and the overall auto industry — with sales projected to slip 2.6% year over year for the quarter, according to Cox Automotive.

raw.headline: Tesla Defies Auto Industry Sales Slump, Goldman Raises Q2 Delivery Forecast

headline: Chips Crack the Rally - Dow Keeps Climbing, SpaceX Passes Amazon
summary: Monday's all-everything rally lasted exactly one session. Tuesday was the kind of split tape that reminds you why an index print can hide more than it reveals, the Dow Jones pushed to another record while the semiconductor complex got taken to the woodshed.

headline: Ford (F) Starts Contract Talks That Could Shape Canada Auto Labor Deals
summary: Ford Motor and Canadian autoworker union Unifor have started what union leaders describe as the most consequential contract talks in a generation. The negotiations are expected to set terms that will influence future labor agreements at General Motors and Stellantis in Canada. Outcomes from these talks could affect Ford's North American operations, labor costs, and competitive position in the region. Ford Motor, ticker NYSE:F, enters these negotiations with its stock at $14.41 and a return...
